core_mechanism: identification of the continuum (scaling) limit of a discrete random path as a conformally invariant random curve obtained by driving a deterministic geometric growth/evolution equation (the Loewner flow) with a one-dimensional Brownian motion of tunable diffusivity
brief_reason: The abstract proves that the lattice-independent scaling limit of loop-erased random walk and the uniform-spanning-tree Peano curve are the radial SLE(2) and chordal SLE(8) paths and are conformally invariant, i.e. it characterizes the discrete process's continuum limit as a Brownian-motion-driven Loewner evolution.
